sections.paragraph:
παρέμπτωσις, εως, ἡ (cp. πτῶσις and the pf. of πίπτω: πέπτωκα; Aristot. et al.) throwing of someth. in besides, insidious plot διά τινας παρεμπτώσεις, in wordplay 1 Cl 51:1 (the word is not found in the Gk. ms. trad., but Clem. Alex. has it, Strom. 4, 113, 1, in a paraphrase of our pass., and the Lat. and Copt. versions of 1 Cl agree w. him. S. Bihlmeyer, app.).—DELG s.v. πίπτω C.

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts
  2. pf. = perfect

Aristot Aristot , various works, IV b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

someth. someth. = something

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

Gk. Gk. = Greek

  1. ms. = manuscript(s)

trad. trad. = tradition

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

Lat. Lat. = Latin

Copt. Copt. = Coptic

Bihlmeyer Bihlmeyer = KB., Die Apostolischen Väter—List 6

app. app. = appendix, apparatus (when ref. to marginal text-critical information, esp. in N.)

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 775
